body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Common Ancestor Objective Function and Parent Set DAG Metric Container Extension
Controlling Secure Network Enrollment in RPL networks

SPEAKER S1: Hello, John. Hey. Yep. Disconnected. I think we can start. Yep. Yeah. Okay. Welcome to the wrong meeting. I apologize for the missing in name of the working group in the title. Um, okay. Uh, please. This, uh, take in consideration the not. Well, we will not read it, but this meeting aligns with the not well of the IETF and their policies.
SPEAKER S2: Uh, you also need, uh, to note that it's being recorded, right, for the people who are remotely.
SPEAKER S1: Sorry.
SPEAKER S2: It's, uh. The meeting is also being recorded for the people who are remotely.
SPEAKER S1: Ah, yes. Yes. Thank you. Yes. So. Yeah. So not the note. Really? Well, that, uh, we need to treat each other with respect. And if you don't feel in that way, please contact the, um, those persons. Um. The tips, please. If you're not talking, mute your microphone. Keep audio and video off. If you are not using the onsite version, um, please join to the meeting in order to register your attendance. Uh, okay. These are the resources. You can utilize it as well from the IETF web page. And, uh, it would be nice if we have some volunteers for the minute takers.
SPEAKER S2: Someone I might take some if I'm not speaking.
SPEAKER S1: Okay. Thank you. Okay. This is the agenda for today. So we will provide an introduction of the working group. The working group status. Then we will mention the status of the active role enrollment priority draft. Then Aris will stay the updates on the initial extension draft. And then we will provide the status and topics. And finally open floor for everyone. Comments or questions about the agenda. Okay. So this is the status of the draft LDB ripple a got comment from John. So the authors are addressing the the issues the Projections was submitted to the ISG. NSC extension is a is in progress will be presented today. Enrollment priority the same. The same. The progression will be presented today. RFD was submitted for publication and we have some drafts that need for attention capabilities. Um string root acknowledged. And this modifications. Then the milestones. We have achieved one milestone from the last time. Um. Um, the we submitted to the ISG the RFD document.
SPEAKER S2: Um.
SPEAKER S1: Okay. We plan a minor rechartering. Actually, our current work items are as follows. Current using RFC 65 five, five three, 6554, and IPv6 in PVC encapsulation. Additional protocol elements to reduce packet size and the amount of required routing states. Automatic selection of MPL forwarders to reduce message replication data models for ripple and MPL management. Multicast enhancement algorithm. So these are the current work items, but we plan to present some minor rechartering. So we plan to delete the guidance in using these Rfc's. Since the the draft is already RPC. We don't need more.
